item.title: Investors buy the dip in both stocks and bonds, BofA says
item.description: Investors took advantage of the drop in stock and bond ​prices in the latest week to ‌snap up both, even though a steep rise in energy prices on the back ​of the war in the Middle ​East rattled sentiment, according to Bank ⁠of America Global Research on Friday.

item.title: Unilever in takeover talks with McCormick for food business
item.description: Unilever said on Friday it was in talks ​with U.S.-based McCormick & Company about selling ‌its foods business, which includes brands such as Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr ​soups and seasonings.

item.title: War in Iran threatens fresh food-price shock across developing world
item.description: Disrupted fertiliser shipments and soaring energy prices from the war in Iran are threatening to unleash a fresh ​food-price surge across vulnerable nations, risking a years-long setback just as many were recovering from successive global shocks.

item.title: China's record oil output reaches limits of what's possible
item.description: China, the world's top oil importer, succeeded in a seven-year campaign to boost its own production, achieving a record high last year with aggressive drilling at ageing fields, an offshore boom and nascent shale oil output.

item.title: Novartis to buy experimental breast cancer drug in up to $3 billion deal
item.description: Swiss drugmaker Novartis on Friday agreed ​to buy a breast cancer drug candidate for up ‌to $3 billion from U.S. biotech firm Synnovation Therapeutics, adding a targeted therapy to its pipeline of cancer drugs.

item.title: WTO reform deadlock may prompt some countries to seek other options on free trade
item.description: Failure to chart a viable reform path for the World Trade Organization at a meeting next week will nudge members to pursue other options to set rules and advance ​free trade, diplomats and officials told Reuters.

item.title: Work from home, avoid air travel to deal with higher energy prices, IEA says
item.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA), which this month agreed a record release of oil from strategic stockpiles to deal ​with the effects of the U.S-Israeli war with ‌Iran, outlined on Friday proposals to ease oil price pressures on consumers, such as working-from-home and avoiding air travel.

item.title: Australia's Star Entertainment names Bally's executive Charles Diao as CFO
item.description: Australian casino operator Star Entertainment ​Group said on Friday it has appointed HC ‌Charles Diao as group chief financial officer, marking another leadership addition from U.S.-based Bally's Corporation .

item.title: Morning Bid: Hawkish rate repricing halts the dollar's rally
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rae Wee

item.title: Virgin Australia to adjust fares to reflect cost pressures
item.description: Virgin Australia is adjusting fares as rising costs across the aviation sector are "exacerbated by ​the situation in the Middle East", ‌the Australian carrier said in a statement to Reuters on Friday.

item.title: J.P. Morgan sees ECB hiking rates in April, July on rising inflation risks
item.description: J.P. Morgan on Friday said it ​expects the European ‌Central Bank (ECB) to deliver two rate hikes in ​April and July, ​in sharp contrast to ⁠its previous forecast ​of steady rates through ​2026, as policymakers signaled at rate hike discussions due ​to rising inflation ​risks.

item.title: 'Great crackdown': Russia tightens the screws on the internet
item.description: Office workers toiling with blocked internet. Teenagers continually forced to switch VPNs. Taxi drivers struggling to find their way around Moscow without online navigation.

item.title: Goldman Sachs expects prolonged BoE pause, sees next rate cut in 2027
item.description: Goldman Sachs has pushed back its forecast for Bank of England interest rate ​cuts to 2027, after the central ‌bank kept rates steady and flagged inflation risks from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: US says it disrupted botnets that infected over 3 million devices worldwide
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department on Thursday said it took part in an operation with ​Germany and Canada to take down infrastructure used by ‌four major botnets that infected more than 3 million devices worldwide, including hundreds of thousands in the U.S.

item.title: Dollar toppled as oil shock turns central banks hawkish
item.description: The dollar slid from multi-month highs this week as soaring energy prices upended the outlook for global interest rates, with the U.S. Federal Reserve left alone as the only major central bank that is not expected to hike rates this ​year.

item.title: Netflix sees more prospects for live events in South Korea after BTS
item.description: Netflix anticipates more opportunities for live events in South Korea, the company said on ​Friday, as the U.S. streaming platform prepares to ‌livestream a highly anticipated BTS comeback concert in Seoul.

item.title: Oil prices retreat, bonds struggle on hawkish rate repricing as Iran war rages
item.description: Oil prices eased on Friday while bonds were nursing losses, after global central bankers sounded the alarm on inflation risks stemming from the ongoing war in ​the Middle East that has sent markets into a tailspin.

item.title: China's Bank of Ningbo readies digital yuan business as Beijing expands e-CNY
item.description: China's Bank of Ningbo said it is inviting suppliers to help build a system to handle ​the digital yuan, in a sign that the country's ‌central bank may soon hand out more licenses as it tries to broaden the use of its e-CNY.

item.title: Exclusive: Tesla in talks with Chinese firms to buy $2.9 bln worth of solar equipment, sources say
item.description: Tesla is looking to buy equipment worth $2.9 billion for manufacturing solar panels and cells from Chinese suppliers including Suzhou Maxwell Technologies , two people familiar with the matter said, ​as CEO Elon Musk aims to add 100 gigawatts of solar capacity in the United States.

item.title: Oil falls as US and allies look to boost supply, unchoke Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices fell on Friday as leading European nations and Japan offered to join efforts to secure safe passage for ​ships through the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. outlined moves ‌to boost oil supply.

item.title: Unilever in talks to combine food business with McCormick, WSJ reports
item.description: Unilever is in talks to separate its food business and combine it ​with spice maker McCormick in an all-stock deal ‌that could come within weeks, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing sources.

item.title: FCC approves combination of Nexstar and Tegna TV stations
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission said on ​Thursday it has approved the sale ‌of some local broadcast TV stations from Tegna to Nexstar .

item.title: Nexstar's $3.5 billion Tegna deal cleared by US DOJ, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has unconditionally cleared Nexstar's $3.5 billion deal to acquire rival ​Tegna , Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing ‌people familiar with the matter.

item.title: OpenAI plans desktop 'superapp' to simplify user experience, WSJ reports
item.description: OpenAI is planning ​to unify ‌its ChatGPT app, coding ​platform Codex ​and browser into ⁠a desktop "superapp" ​to simplify ​user experience, the Wall Street ​Journal reported ​on Thursday.

item.title: US charges three people with conspiring to divert AI tech to China
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department said on Thursday that three ‌people have been charged with conspiring to unlawfully divert U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China.

item.title: Locked out BP workers picket outside Indiana refinery amid labor contract dispute
item.description: Workers ‌at BP’s Whiting, Indiana, refinery began picketing on Thursday after the British oil major barred more than 800 union members from entering the facility, following months of stalled ​contract negotiations.

item.title: High-speed combat drone production starts at new US Anduril plant in days
item.description: Anduril Industries will begin building its new FURY, "loyal wingman," high-speed combat drones ‌in the coming days at a new facility in Ohio, as the U.S. military's interest in unmanned aircraft surges following battlefield successes in Ukraine and Iran.

item.title: US SEC forming new team to police accounting issues
item.description: Wall Street's top regulator is forming a new ‌enforcement unit for accounting matters while cutting staff at an outside watchdog formed to police the accounting industry in the wake of the Enron-era scandals of the early 2000s, according to job postings and a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Nvidia to sell 1 million chips to Amazon by end of 2027 in cloud deal
item.description: Nvidia will sell 1 million of its graphics processing unit chips, along with a host of the AI giant's other ​offerings, to Amazon.com's cloud computing unit by 2027, a Nvidia executive ‌told Reuters on Thursday.

item.title: Prices for oil, fuel cargoes smash record highs as Iran war chokes Middle East supply
item.description: If you want to buy a cargo of oil in Asia or jet fuel in ​Europe right now, you may have to pay a record price for it.
